goal: Close the Phase-1 gate's top blocker by wiring an `mcp` subcommand into `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` so `ucil-daemon mcp --stdio` invokes `McpServer::new().serve(tokio::io::stdin(), tokio::io::stdout())` — which is exactly the remediation the `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh` self-diagnostic prints on current main (see integration report 2026-04-18T20:29:02Z and effectiveness report 2026-04-18T20:30:45Z).
plan_summary: All 34 Phase-1 features are flipped `passes=true`, but `scripts/gate/phase-1.sh` is still RED because `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh` exits 1: the binary spawned via `target/debug/ucil-daemon mcp --stdio` emits 0 bytes because `main.rs` is still the Phase-0 skeleton (it only calls `tracing_subscriber::fmt::init()` and returns). The fix is ~10 lines of source edit: branch on `std::env::args().nth(1).as_deref()`, and if the first positional is `"mcp"`, construct `McpServer::new()` (stub mode, KG bootstrap deferred to a later WO) and drive `serve(stdin, stdout)` to EOF; otherwise fall through to the existing Phase-0 path so the daemon-mode entry (to be wired in a future WO) is unaffected. A critical sub-point: in `mcp` mode the tracing subscriber MUST write to `stderr` (default writer is `stdout` which would pollute the JSON-RPC newline-delimited stream the e2e script parses). A new integration test `crates/ucil-daemon/tests/e2e_mcp_stdio.rs` spawns the binary via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon")` and replays the same `initialize` + `tools/list` handshake as `e2e-mcp-smoke.sh`, asserting 22 tools + CEQP universal params on each, so regression pressure lives inside the crate's own test suite and does not depend on the shell script.
scope_in: crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs — replace the 4-line Phase-0 skeleton with an async dispatch on `std::env::args().nth(1).as_deref()`. If the first positional argument is `Some("mcp")`, initialise `tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_writer(std::io::stderr).init()` (stderr-directed so stdout stays pristine for JSON-RPC framing), construct `ucil_daemon::server::McpServer::new()`, and call `.serve(tokio::io::stdin(), tokio::io::stdout()).await` — propagating the `McpError` via `anyhow::Result` with a `.context("ucil-daemon mcp --stdio: serve loop terminated with error")`. On clean EOF the future resolves `Ok(())` and the process returns 0. Any trailing positional arguments (e.g. `--stdio`) are silently ignored on this WO — they do not route to a different transport and are accepted purely for CLI-shape compatibility with the e2e script. If the first positional is `None` or anything other than `"mcp"`, preserve the existing Phase-0 behavior byte-for-byte: `tracing_subscriber::fmt::init()` (stdout-default; will be tightened in a future daemon-mode WO) + `tracing::info!(version = ucil_core::VERSION, "ucil-daemon starting")` + `Ok(())`. Do NOT introduce clap / structopt / argh — `std::env::args().nth(1).as_deref()` is sufficient for a single subcommand dispatch and avoids a new workspace dep.

crates/ucil-daemon/tests/e2e_mcp_stdio.rs — NEW integration-test file. Test `e2e_mcp_stdio_handshake_returns_22_tools_with_ceqp`: spawn the binary via `std::process::Command::new(env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon")).arg("mcp").arg("--stdio")` with `.stdin(Stdio::piped()).stdout(Stdio::piped()).stderr(Stdio::null())`; write the two JSON-RPC lines (`initialize` with protocolVersion `"2024-11-05"` matching the e2e script exactly, then `tools/list`) to stdin; close stdin; read stdout until EOF or a 15-second wall-clock bound via `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(15), ...)` wrapping a `spawn_blocking` read OR — simpler and acceptable for a one-shot integration test — use `std::process::Child::wait_with_output()` with a child-side exit guaranteed by the `read_line → EOF` loop inside `McpServer::serve`. Parse each stdout line as `serde_json::Value`; assert `responses.iter().find(|r| r["id"] == 2).is_some()`; on the `tools/list` response assert `result.tools.as_array().unwrap().len() == 22`, then assert each of the 22 frozen tool names from `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh` is present in `result.tools[].name`, and finally assert each of the 4 CEQP keys (`reason`, `current_task`, `files_in_context`, `token_budget`) is a property on every tool's `inputSchema.properties`. The test is `#[test]` (not `#[tokio::test]` — the binary has its own runtime) and lives at module root per DEC-0005 (no `mod tests { }` wrapper). It does NOT start docker, does NOT rely on Serena, does NOT rely on a populated KG — the `McpServer::new()` stub path is all that is exercised, consistent with the e2e-mcp-smoke contract.

crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml [dev-dependencies] — add `serde_json = { workspace = true }` if not already present under dev-dependencies (it IS already present under `[dependencies]` at line 28, but the integration test in `tests/` directory is a separate compile unit and pulls from `[dev-dependencies]` OR from `[dependencies]` via re-export. Re-reading: Cargo DOES allow integration tests to use a crate's runtime deps directly. If the test compiles without adding serde_json to dev-deps, leave dev-deps unchanged. The executor verifies by running `cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio` and adding the dev-dep only if the compile fails with `unresolved import serde_json`). `tempfile.workspace = true` at line 65 is already present and not needed here.

Rustdoc on `main`: replace the `//! Phase 0 skeleton — starts up and exits cleanly.` header with `//! \`ucil-daemon\` entry point. Dispatches on the first positional argument: \"mcp\" routes to the MCP stdio server; anything else runs the (future) daemon mode.` (module rustdoc). Reference master-plan §18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1744 (`ucil-daemon: basic MCP server over stdio`). The 22-tool inventory and CEQP param requirements flow from master-plan §3 (frozen tool surface) and §3.1 (CEQP universal params). Do NOT reference internal types in the rustdoc unless via fully qualified paths — past WOs (WO-0009 / WO-0025 / WO-0027 / WO-0039 lessons) have been rejected for rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links.

Commit ladder — approximately 3 commits: (1) `feat(daemon): wire mcp --stdio subcommand into main.rs` (the main.rs edit, ~15 LOC touched); (2) `test(daemon): e2e integration test — spawn binary, assert 22 tools + CEQP` (the new test file, ~80-120 LOC under DEC-0005 module-introduction precedent); (3, only if needed) `build(daemon): add serde_json to dev-dependencies for e2e_mcp_stdio test` (Cargo.toml dev-dep bump). Soft target ≤50 LOC/commit per `.claude/rules/commit-style.md`; the test-introduction commit MAY exceed under the same module-introduction precedent that permitted oversized test commits in WO-0006 / WO-0017 / WO-0039.
scope_out: NO KG bootstrap in `mcp` mode — `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(kg)` is NOT called; `McpServer::new()` is correct for this WO. Every `tools/call` in stdio mode correctly returns the `_meta.not_yet_implemented: true` stub envelope per phase-1 invariant #9, matching the behavior unit-tested by `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` and the selector `server::*` suite that already passes on main. End-to-end `find_definition` over stdio with real symbol data is a LATER WO (out of scope here because it requires reading the target repo path from CLI args, bootstrapping a SQLite KG, running the tree-sitter extraction pipeline over the fixture at startup, and wiring `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph(Arc::new(Mutex::new(kg)))` — all of which are a superset of this WO).

NO daemon-mode wiring — the non-`mcp` arm stays on the Phase-0 `tracing_subscriber::fmt::init(); tracing::info!(...); Ok(())` path. Daemon mode (PID file, SIGTERM/SIGHUP handling, file watcher startup, plugin manager spawn) already exists in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs` (WO-0021) and `crates/ucil-daemon/src/startup.rs` (WO-0028) but wiring those into `main.rs` from a CLI flag is a future WO outside this scope.
NO clap / structopt / argh / pico-args dep added to the workspace or to `ucil-daemon` — `std::env::args().nth(1).as_deref()` is sufficient for the single-subcommand dispatch on this WO.
NO edit to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` — the `McpServer::serve` API is already stable and exercised by in-process tests. This WO treats `server.rs` as read-only.

NO edit to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` — `pub mod server;` and the other re-exports are already in place; the new test uses `ucil_daemon::server::McpServer` via the crate's existing public surface if referenced, but the integration test deliberately does NOT link against the library (it spawns the binary).

NO edit to `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh`, `scripts/gate/phase-1.sh`, `scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh`, `scripts/verify/diagnostics-bridge.sh`, `scripts/verify/multi-lang-coverage.sh`, `scripts/verify/serena-live.sh`, or any other `scripts/` file — the gate shape is frozen and the fix lives in source. Separately, `scripts/verify/diagnostics-bridge.sh` and `scripts/verify/multi-lang-coverage.sh` remain RED and are tracked as distinct follow-on items (harness fixes or their own narrow WOs); this WO does not try to unblock them.
NO feature-list flip expected — all 34 Phase-1 features are already `passes=true`. This WO's purpose is gate-closure, not feature delivery. `feature_ids: []` accordingly.
NO `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, `xfail`, `it.skip`, commented-out assertions, `todo!()`, `unimplemented!()`, `pass`-only test bodies — forbidden by root CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.
NO retry on watchman / session_manager / watcher source — WO-0039 already landed those per DEC-0011; this WO does not touch them.

NO changes to existing daemon modules other than `main.rs`: `lifecycle.rs`, `plugin_manager.rs`, `server.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `session_ttl.rs`, `storage.rs`, `executor.rs`, `startup.rs`, `priority_queue.rs`, `text_search.rs`, `understand_code.rs`, `watcher.rs`, `test_support.rs`. Only `main.rs` + the new `tests/e2e_mcp_stdio.rs` + possibly a one-line `Cargo.toml` dev-dep addition are in scope.
acceptance: `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh` exits 0 on a freshly-built `target/debug/ucil-daemon` — 22 tools registered, CEQP params present on every tool.
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio` exits 0 with exactly one passing integration test (`e2e_mcp_stdio_handshake_returns_22_tools_with_ceqp`).
`cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib` still exits 0 with no failures (WO-0039's DEC-0011-fenced suite still clean; no regression in session_manager / watcher tests).
`cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean — no new warnings introduced by the main.rs rewrite.
`cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` exits 0 with no `^error` and no `^warning: unresolved` lines (rustdoc-link landmine guard, per the WO-0009 / WO-0025 / WO-0039 pattern).

`./target/debug/ucil-daemon` with NO arguments still exits 0 in under 1 s and prints no JSON on stdout (backward compatibility with the Phase-0 default-arm behavior; verifier greps stdout for JSON and asserts empty).

Mutation check (verifier runs): stash the `.serve(stdin, stdout).await` call in `main.rs` (replace with `Ok(())`) → `e2e_mcp_stdio_handshake_returns_22_tools_with_ceqp` fails AND `scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh` exits 1; restore → both green. This proves the new main.rs dispatch is the sole reason the gate moves.

Phase-1 gate sub-check `[OK] MCP 22 tools registered` appears in `scripts/gate/phase-1.sh` output (previously `[FAIL]`). The other sub-checks (`[FAIL] diagnostics bridge live`, `[FAIL] multi-lang probes`, 3× `[FAIL] coverage gate:`) are NOT required to flip on this WO — they are tracked separately.
acceptance_criteria: cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo build -p ucil-daemon --bin ucil-daemon --quiet 2>&1 | tee /tmp/build-WO-0040.log && test -x ./target/debug/ucil-daemon
cd $REPO_ROOT && scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh 2>&1 | tee /tmp/e2e-WO-0040.log && grep -q 'OK — 22 tools registered' /tmp/e2e-WO-0040.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test -p ucil-daemon --test e2e_mcp_stdio 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-e2e-WO-0040.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed' /tmp/test-e2e-WO-0040.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo test -p ucil-daemon --lib 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-lib-WO-0040.log && grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. [0-9]+ passed; 0 failed' /tmp/test-lib-WO-0040.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon --all-targets -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy-WO-0040.log && ! grep -q '^error' /tmp/clippy-WO-0040.log
cd $REPO_ROOT && cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps 2>&1 | tee /tmp/doc-WO-0040.log && ! grep -qE '^(error|warning: unresolved)' /tmp/doc-WO-0040.log
context_refs: master-plan:§3 (frozen 22-tool surface — FROZEN_TOOLS list in scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh mirrors this)
master-plan:§3.1 (CEQP universal params on every tool: reason, current_task, files_in_context, token_budget)
master-plan:§18 Phase 1 Week 3 line 1744 (`ucil-daemon: basic MCP server over stdio (all 22 tools registered, stubs ok)`)
rules:.claude/rules/rust-style.md (thiserror in libs, anyhow in binaries with .context(); tokio::process::Command in async paths; every public item gets rustdoc; no `unwrap()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`)
rules:.claude/rules/commit-style.md (~50 LOC soft target; Conventional Commits with Phase/Feature/Work-order trailers; no --amend after push)

phase-log:ucil-build/phase-log/01-phase-1/CLAUDE.md (invariant #9 — stubs for unimplemented MCP tools MUST return `{"_meta":{"not_yet_implemented":true}}`; invariant #6 — MCP server communicates over stdout/stdin using JSON-RPC 2.0, no HTTP in Phase 1)
verification-report:ucil-build/verification-reports/phase-1-integration.md (2026-04-18T20:29:02Z FAIL verdict cites this exact remediation — `main.rs is still the Phase-0 skeleton`)
verification-report:ucil-build/verification-reports/effectiveness-phase-1.md (2026-04-18T20:30:45Z advisory also cites the same fix: `Wire the stdio MCP subcommand`)
decision:DEC-0005-WO-0006-module-coherence-commits.md (module-introduction commits may exceed the ~50 LOC soft target; applies to the test-introduction commit)

decision:DEC-0010-tests-integration-workspace-crate.md (integration tests at crate-root tests/ directory are the preferred location for per-crate black-box tests — this WO's new e2e_mcp_stdio.rs follows that convention)
gate-script:scripts/verify/e2e-mcp-smoke.sh (the black-box contract this WO must satisfy; the script's error message at line 83-88 literally prints the remediation code this WO implements)

predecessor:ucil-build/work-orders/0010-plugin-lifecycle-and-mcp-server.json (WO-0010 implemented `McpServer::serve` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` — the library API this WO wires into the binary entry point; `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` already asserts the 22-tool stub behavior in-process and will not be disturbed by this WO)

dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W3-F07: passes=true. WO-0010 landed `McpServer::serve(reader, writer)` at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:420` which is already exercised by `server::test_all_22_tools_registered` in-process and returns the 22 `ToolDescriptor`s from `ucil_tools()` with CEQP params per §3.1. The only missing piece is the binary-entry dispatch — which is exactly this WO's scope.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F05: passes=true. `handle_find_definition` exists in server.rs starting line 601 and is dispatched by `McpServer::with_knowledge_graph`. This WO deliberately does NOT boot the KG, so `find_definition` via stdio falls through to the stub envelope — matching the phase-1 invariant #9 behavior that every other non-KG tool already uses. Future WO will boot the KG in stdio mode for real end-to-end navigation.
dependency_readiness_proof.P1-W4-F06: passes=true. CEQP universal params are already embedded in every `ToolDescriptor` emitted by `ucil_tools()` (frozen in `ucil-core::tools`). The new integration test asserts these pass through the stdio transport unchanged.

lessons_applied: Past WO rustdoc intra-doc-link rejections (WO-0009, WO-0025, WO-0027, WO-0039): acceptance_criteria includes a mandatory `cargo doc -p ucil-daemon --no-deps` sub-check that greps for `^error` OR `^warning: unresolved` — caught pre-verifier so no RCF lap is needed.

WO-0038 / WO-0039 grep-for-summary-line regex drift: acceptance_criteria uses `grep -Eq` with alternation that matches BOTH cargo-test (`test result: ok. N passed; 0 failed`) AND cargo-nextest (`N tests run: M passed`) summary-line shapes — no output-shape mismatch.

WO-0027 coverage-gate vs nextest split (DEC-0011): this WO does not mutate PATH or spawn git in any new test, so the DEC-0011 PATH/env guard pattern is not needed. The new test uses `CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon` (cargo-injected absolute path) which is immune to PATH mutation in other tests. `test_support::env_guard` / `PathRestoreGuard` are out of scope for this WO — they are used ONLY by watcher and session_manager tests.

Stdout-pollution trap from tracing subscriber default: in `mcp` mode, `tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_writer(std::io::stderr).init()` is REQUIRED — the default writer is `std::io::stdout()` which would interleave INFO-level span enters with the JSON-RPC response frames and break the e2e script's `jq` parser. Explicitly called out in scope_in and acceptance_criteria.

WO-0024 abandoned rustdoc fix pattern: the module-level rustdoc on `main` is kept SHORT (one sentence) and does NOT reference internal types by 2-letter links — this avoids the `#![deny(rustdoc::broken_intra_doc_links)]` trap that repeatedly caught past WOs.

DEC-0005 module-root flat test style: the new integration test is `#[test] fn e2e_mcp_stdio_handshake_returns_22_tools_with_ceqp()` at module root — NO `mod tests { }` wrapper. Matches the 8 WO-0026 watcher tests and the WO-0039 pattern.

WO-0012 / WO-0020 abandonment avoidance: the test does NOT depend on an external service (no docker, no Serena, no LSP server, no pyright) — the binary is spawned via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_ucil-daemon")` which cargo always provides. Running in any CI environment yields the same result.

Empty feature_ids WO precedent: this WO ships gate-closure work with NO feature flip expected — the WO-0025 fix-incremental-rustdoc-ambiguity and WO-0007 fix-storage-test-selector precedents show that empty-feature WOs are a legitimate shape for closing concrete gate blockers without expanding the feature oracle.
